_comment: Synthetic activation-delta fixtures for task-drift monitor tests. Each example represents before/after activation vectors at a CAPCOM-gate boundary. Vectors are 8-dimensional for test compactness (production uses 768+ dims). Methodology: Abdelnabi et al. 2024 (arXiv:2406.00799).

examples.label: clean
examples.description: Nearly-identical activations across gate boundary — no injection signal.

examples.label: drift
examples.description: Prompt-injection-style activation shift — large delta in several dimensions, simulating adversarial instruction injection at a gate boundary.

examples.label: suspicious
examples.description: Moderate activation shift — between clean and drift bands, possibly an early-stage injection or model-temperature artifact.

examples.label: drift
examples.description: High-magnitude injection: goal-substitution pattern where the after-activation cluster moves toward an entirely different task-direction vector.

examples.label: clean
examples.description: Noise-level delta only — within expected variance of a stable gate crossing.
